reason: covers extension visibility, SysvMessageQueue handles, queue existence, serialized and raw payload send/receive, metadata, settings, and removal semantics
covers basic queue creation, serialized send/receive, stat metadata, and removal through a php-src sibling ftok path
covers corrupted raw message receive when PHP unserialization is requested
covers SysvMessageQueue object display without PHP-visible internal properties
covers msg_set_queue() and msg_stat_queue() permission, uid, gid, and byte-limit metadata
covers sysvmsg false, errno, and warning behavior after queue removal
covers raw non-serialized string, int, float, bool, null, and array message payload behavior
covers msg_send() serialization failure propagation when __serialize() returns a non-array value
